relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Zophar entered with a sharper edge.
He treated Job’s many words as needing correction.
He heard Job’s lament as empty mockery.
He accused Job of claiming purity.
He wished God would speak against Job.
Zophar claimed hidden wisdom would show Job was receiving less than deserved.
He named divine mystery as beyond searching.
Higher than heaven and deeper than Sheol, wisdom exceeds Job’s reach.
It stretches beyond earth and sea.
No one can turn back divine action.
The Divine sees emptiness and harm.
Zophar mocks empty-headed humanity.
He calls Job to prepare his heart and reach toward God.
He says harm and injustice must be put away.
Then, he promises, Job’s face will lift without fear.
Trouble would pass like water.
Darkness would turn into morning.
Hope would become security and rest.
Safety would return, and others would seek his favor.
But the wicked, Zophar says, lose sight, escape, and hope.
